text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of code without changing its external behavior. The main goal is to make the code cleaner, more readable, and easier to maintain. For example, you might rename confusing variables, break long functions into smaller parts, or simplify complex conditions. Refactoring does not add new features or fix bugs; it improves the design of existing code. This practice helps reduce "technical debt" — the extra work caused by messy code over time. By refactoring regularly, developers save time in the long run and make future changes safer and faster.
